findings.evidence: `JHolomorphic.lean:87-95` defines `IsJHolomorphicWithinAt`/`IsJHolomorphicOn`, but `JHolomorphic.lean:192-225` only provides at/global composition; Mathlib’s corresponding API is setwise first: `Mathlib/Analysis/Calculus/FDeriv/Comp.lean:74` has `HasFDerivWithinAt.comp ... (hst : MapsTo f s t)` and `Comp.lean:194` has `DifferentiableOn.comp ... (st : MapsTo f s t)`.
findings.fix: Prove `IsJHolomorphicWithinAt.comp` with `(hg : IsJHolomorphicWithinAt J' J'' g t (f x))`, `(hf : IsJHolomorphicWithinAt J J' f s x)`, and `(hst : MapsTo f s t)`, then derive `IsJHolomorphicOn.comp`, `IsJHolomorphicAt.comp`, and global `comp` as special cases.
findings.issue: Composition is proved only for `IsJHolomorphicAt` and then global maps, leaving the introduced `WithinAt`/`On` API without the natural setwise chain rule.

summary: The new predicates are mostly at the intended fixed-linear-structure level, but the composition API is under-generalized for the setwise predicates introduced in the same file.
